extra.message: User input from request.form.get('username') and request.form.get('password') is concatenated directly into an SQL query string via f-string interpolation and passed to cursor.execute() without parameterization. This allows SQL injection.
Hardcoded credentials USERNAME='admin' and PASSWORD='password123' are stored in plaintext in the source code, exposing secrets to anyone with access to the codebase.
User password 'password1' is inserted into the database as plaintext without any hashing or encryption, exposing sensitive credentials if the database is compromised.

cwe: CWE-256: Plaintext Storage of a Password

extra.message: User input from request.args.get('q') is directly embedded into an HTML response without any sanitization or escaping, allowing reflected cross-site scripting (XSS).

The dashboard route renders a cookie value (session) directly into an HTML response without sanitization. An attacker can set the cookie to arbitrary JavaScript via the login form (or SQL injection) and trigger XSS on the victim's browser.
The /dashboard route has no authentication check. It reads a plaintext cookie ('session') which any attacker can forge by setting their own cookie to any username, bypassing the login entirely.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Session cookie is set to a plaintext username without any signing, encryption, HttpOnly flag, or Secure attribute. The cookie is trivially forgeable and accessible to JavaScript (enabling session theft via XSS).

cwe: CWE-614: Sensitive Cookie in HTTPS Session Without 'Secure' Attribute

extra.message: The /login POST endpoint has no CSRF protection (no CSRF token validation or anti-forgery mechanism). The login form in app/templates/index.html:9 also lacks a CSRF token. An attacker can forge cross-site requests to log users in as attacker-controlled accounts.

The Flask application is bound to port 80 with host='0.0.0.0' without any TLS/HTTPS configuration. All traffic, including credentials submitted via the login form and session cookies, is transmitted in cleartext.

cwe: CWE-319: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information
